title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make using just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. This salt-cured fermentation method takes about 10 minutes to prep and four weeks to cure, resulting in a highly citrusy, umami-rich ingredient that adds intense flavor to any dish.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Start packing the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible; lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice, adding juice from an extra lemon if needed.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.

Once opened, store in the refrigerator indefinitely; rinse off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes, and discard the pulp if desired, using the rinsed rind anywhere you would use lemon zest.
